ucs.title: [LLM] Poseidon Group IGT-style domain reconnaissance burst from Office/script parent
ucs.description: Detects the rapid sequential execution of net.exe/systeminfo/ipconfig/whoami/tasklist/netstat from a single host that is the signature behavior of Poseidon Group's Information Gathering Toolkit (IGT). Kaspersky's reporting on the Portuguese-speaking Poseidon Group highlights that, after spear-phishing delivery, their post-exploitation modules execute a near-canonical battery of Windows discovery commands to enumerate domain admins, network shares, sessions, and processes before exfiltrating the data used in their later blackmail/extortion business model.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents dc(Processes.process_name) as distinct_disco_bins from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","whoami.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","qprocess.exe","query.exe","arp.exe","route.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval net_user=if(match(cmdlines,"net(1)?\.exe\s+user"),1,0), net_group=if(match(cmdlines,"net(1)?\.exe\s+(group|localgroup)"),1,0), net_view=if(match(cmdlines,"net(1)?\.exe\s+view"),1,0), net_session=if(match(cmdlines,"net(1)?\.exe\s+(session|use|share)"),1,0), nltest_dom=if(match(cmdlines,"nltest.*(domain_trusts|dclist)"),1,0) | eval poseidon_disco_score = distinct_disco_bins + net_user + net_group + net_view + net_session + nltest_dom | where poseidon_disco_score >= 7 AND match(parents,"(?i)(powershell|cmd|wscript|cscript|winword|excel|outlook|mshta|wmiprvse)\.exe") | sort - poseidon_disco_score | table _time dest user parents distinct_disco_bins poseidon_disco_score cmdlines
ucs.kql:
let window = 10m;
let disco_bins = dynamic(["net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","ipconfig.exe","whoami.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","qprocess.exe","query.exe","arp.exe","route.exe"]);
let suspect_parents = dynamic(["powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","mshta.exe","wmiprvse.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ (disco_bins)
| extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| extend bucket = bin(Timestamp, window)
| summarize
    distinct_bins = dcount(FileName),
    cmds = make_set(cmd, 30),
    parents = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 10),
    first_seen = min(Timestamp),
    last_seen = max(Timestamp),
    accounts = make_set(AccountName, 5)
    by DeviceId, DeviceName, bucket
| extend
    net_user    = iff(cmds has_any ("net user","net1 user"), 1, 0),
    net_group   = iff(cmds has_any ("net group","net localgroup","net1 group"), 1, 0),
    net_view    = iff(cmds has_any ("net view","net1 view"), 1, 0),
    net_session = iff(cmds has_any ("net session","net use","net share"), 1, 0),
    nltest_dom  = iff(cmds has_any ("/domain_trusts","/dclist","/dsgetdc"), 1, 0)
| extend poseidon_disco_score = distinct_bins + net_user + net_group + net_view + net_session + nltest_dom
| where poseidon_disco_score >= 7
| mv-expand p = parents to typeof(string)
| where p in~ (suspect_parents)
| project first_seen, last_seen, DeviceName, accounts, parents, distinct_bins, poseidon_disco_score, cmds
| order by poseidon_disco_score desc
ucs.rationale: Generic 'net user' detection is noisy, but Poseidon's IGT runs essentially the entire discovery battery in one short burst from a script/Office parent — a fingerprint Kaspersky documented as their post-implant trademark. By scoring on (a) the count of distinct discovery binaries, (b) presence of the specific net-subcommand cluster they use to enumerate DAs/shares/sessions, and (c) a non-interactiv

ucs.title: [LLM] Poseidon Group masquerading binary (Match Legitimate Name) executing from non-System32 path with credential-access child
ucs.description: Hunts for Poseidon Group's documented tradecraft of dropping implants named after legitimate Windows system binaries (svchost.exe, lsass.exe, services.exe, etc.) into user-writable directories, then using them to launch credential dumping or PowerShell. This combines T1036.005 (Match Legitimate Name) with T1003 (OS Credential Dumping) and T1059.001 in a way that maps directly to the Poseidon implants Kaspersky detailed in the Operation Poseidon report.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.process_path) as path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("svchost.exe","lsass.exe","csrss.exe","winlogon.exe","services.exe","spoolsv.exe","smss.exe","wininit.exe","conhost.exe","taskhost.exe","taskhostw.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=path "(?i)^(?<root>[a-z]:\\\\[^\\\\]+)" | where NOT match(path,"(?i)\\\\Windows\\\\(System32|SysWOW64|WinSxS)\\\\") AND match(path,"(?i)\\\\(Users|AppData|ProgramData|Temp|Public|PerfLogs|\\$Recycle\\.Bin|Windows\\\\(Debug|Tasks|Fonts|Tracing))\\\\") | rename process_id as masq_pid process_name as masq_name | join type=inner dest masq_pid [ | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as child_cmd values(Processes.process_name) as child_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("svchost.exe","lsass.exe","csrss.exe","winlogon.exe","services.exe","spoolsv.exe","smss.exe","wininit.exe","conhost.exe","taskhost.exe","taskhostw.exe") (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wmic.exe") OR Processes.process="*lsass*" OR Processes.process="*sekurlsa*" OR Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" OR Processes.process="*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*ntds.dit*" OR Processes.process="*vssadmin*" OR Processes.process="*-enc*" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadString*") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename parent_process_id as masq_pid ] | table _time dest user masq_name path child_name child_cmd
ucs.kql:
let masq_names = dynamic(["svchost.exe","lsass.exe","csrss.exe","winlogon.exe","services.exe","spoolsv.exe","smss.exe","wininit.exe","conhost.exe","taskhost.exe","taskhostw.exe"]);
let legit_paths = dynamic([@"\windows\system32\", @"\windows\syswow64\", @"\windows\winsxs\"]);
let writable_paths = dynamic([@"\users\", @"\appdata\", @"\programdata\", @"\temp\", @"\public\", @"\perflogs\", @"\$recycle.bin\", @"\windows\debug\", @"\windows\tasks\", @"\windows\fonts\", @"\windows\tracing\"]);
let cred_keywords = dynamic(["lsass","sekurlsa","invoke-mimikatz","out-minidump","comsvcs.dll","minidump","samdump","ntds.dit","vssadmin","shadowcopy","procdump","dumpcreds","-encodedcommand","-enc ","frombase64string","downloadstring","iex(","invoke-expression","webclient"]);
let masq_procs = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where FileName in~ (masq_names)
    | extend lp = tolower(FolderPath)
    | where not(lp has_any (legit_paths))
    | where lp has_any (writable_paths)
    | project MasqTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, MasqName = FileName, MasqPath = FolderPath, MasqPID = ProcessId, MasqSHA256 = SHA256;
let cred_children = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (masq_names)
    | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wmic.exe")
        or tolower(ProcessCommandLine) has_any (cred_keywords)
    | project ChildTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, MasqPID = InitiatingProcessId, ChildName = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
masq_procs
| join kind=inner cred_children on DeviceId, MasqPID
| where ChildTime between (MasqTime .. MasqTime + 1h)
| project MasqTime, ChildTime, DeviceName, AccountName, MasqName, MasqPath, MasqSHA256, ChildName, ChildCmd
| order by MasqTime desc
ucs.rationale: Poseidon Group is one of the few crews where 'svchost.exe running from C:\Users\...' is a documented payload-naming convention rather than a one-off observation — Kaspersky specifically called out their use of Windows-binary-name masquerading. Pure masquerading detection alone fires on PUPs/installers, so this rule chains the masquerading process to a credential-access or encoded-PowerShell child
